rationale: Fidelity survived the fix round; my only round-1 advisory (R8 caller-side wiring built but neither rendered nor deferred) is now fully resolved and maps to spec R8 exactly. Spec R8 names precisely two caller surfaces -- 'mindspec config show and the in-binary complete-gate wiring' -- and Bead 4 now owns BOTH: Step 3 renders the note in config show (TestConfigShow_ReviewerCountNoteWhenPanelDiffers), Step 4 wires it into internal/complete's advisory path (TestPanelAdvisory_ReviewerCountNote), and a new Provenance row + AC bind both to Bead 4. No third surface is invented; no requirement dropped. I verified every new wiring target is real: internal/complete/panel_advisory.go with the panelScanFn/panelTallyFn/panelAdvisoryOut seams (lines 28-30), complete.go:319 holds panelReg and a loaded config (gateCfg at :311) at the gate site, panel.Panel.ExpectedReviewers exists (panel.go:54), ApproveThreshold() single home at panel.go:76, gate.go:257 retains 'threshold > 0'. Leaf invariant holds -- ReviewerCountNote(recorded, configDefault int) takes plain ints, both callers pass them; both steps are advisory-only with PanelGateDecision and every Allow/Block untouched. Bead 4 frontmatter now lists panel_advisory.go(+_test) + workflow/interfaces.md with depends_on [2,3]; the Testing Strategy soundly justifies grouping both consumers into Bead 4 to preserve the 2||3 parallelism and the leaf helper's clean separation. Making config show scan real panels (not defaults-only) is a more faithful reading of R8 than the deferral I proposed. Out-of-Scope still respected.

findings.summary: Round-1 advisory ADDRESSED: both R8 caller surfaces are now wired into Bead 4 with tests, doc-sync, and grounded targets.
findings.detail: My round-1 approve carried one advisory: panel.ReviewerCountNote was built + unit-tested in Bead 3 but no bead rendered it from config show or the complete gate, and the plan was silent (neither wiring nor an explicit deferral). The round-2 plan (d53c5939) fixes this by making Bead 4 the single owner of both caller-side surfaces R8 names. Step 3: config show's command handler scans registered panels (panel.Scan/panel.ForBead) and appends ReviewerCountNote when a recorded expected_reviewers differs from cfg.PanelExpectedReviewers() -- the scan/append lives in the handler, NOT renderConfig, and the command still writes no file (R9 read-only preserved; verified by the git-status-clean grep). Step 4: the complete-gate advisory path in panel_advisory.go computes ReviewerCountNote(panelReg.Panel.ExpectedReviewers, cfg.PanelExpectedReviewers()) and prints it advisory-only -- PanelGateDecision untouched, Allow/Block unchanged (asserted by TestPanelAdvisory_ReviewerCountNote plus the whole-package green). I confirmed the wiring is grounded, not invented: panel_advisory.go and its panelScanFn/panelTallyFn/panelAdvisoryOut seams exist; complete.go already holds panelReg and a loaded config at the gate call site; panel.Panel.ExpectedReviewers is a real field. Frontmatter key_file_paths + the workflow/interfaces.md doc-sync + depends_on [2,3] all match the added work. This is a stronger fidelity outcome than the deferral I suggested -- the plan honors R8's literal naming of config show by rendering the note there coherently (only when a scanned panel diverges), rather than deferring it.
